t_ch: 你在这里做什么？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 哦！又是一位迷失之人。不要担心，不要着急，我会为你指引道路。
t_en: Oh! Here's another lost soul. Don't worry. I'll show you the way.

t_ch: 我叫卡波特，是界种科的科员。但还有另一重身份，更为重要——
t_en: My name is Capote. I'm a member of the Department of Ecology, but my other identity is even more important...

t_ch: 那就是黑塔大人最忠实的追随者！
t_en: I am a die-hard fan of Madam Herta!

t_ch: 我在这里，就是想要为这座空间站，也是为了黑塔大人，做出一点微薄的努力。
t_en: I'm here to make my humble contributions to this space station, as well as to Madam Herta.

t_ch: 虽然我不是保卫科的成员，没有健硕的体格去与孽障抗争。
t_en: Although I'm not a member of the Security Department and I'm not strong enough to fight against evil...

t_ch: 但我会通过我的语言，去指引每一个，就像是你这样，迷失在慌乱之中的人。
t_en: But with my words, I will guide all those who are lost and in a panic like yourself.

t_ch: 你能为我提供什么帮助吗？
t_en: How can you help me?

t_ch: 帮助？对没错，你看上去的确需要一些小小的帮助。
t_en: Help? Right, you do look like you need some help.

t_ch: 从你的眼神里，我能看出焦躁不安。这是一种来源于恐惧的情绪，我能理解。
t_en: I can see anxiety in your eyes. This emotion originates from fear. I can understand it.

t_ch: 而我，将会为你拨开这层心灵上的迷雾。这也是我能为黑塔大人，做出的微小贡献。
t_en: And I will clear the uncertainty in your heart. This is the least I can do for Madam Herta.

t_ch: 嗯……如果你找不到避难所的话就跟着指引方向前进，走那边可以前往主城安全区，我说得清楚吗？
t_en: Well... If you can't find a shelter, follow these directions. You will arrive at the safe zone of the main town if you go that way. Am I making myself clear?

t_ch: 我还有些事情需要问你…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 不然你还是和我一起去避难吧？
t_en: Why don't you take refuge with me?

t_ch: 不，我已经宣誓，用我的灵魂宣誓——必须踏过我的身体，才可入侵这里的生灵！
t_en: No, I took my vows. I swore on my soul — If anyone is to harm the creatures here, they'll have to step over my dead body.

t_ch: 在这场风暴结束之前，我绝不会，没错，卡波特绝不会前往避难！
t_en: Before this storm is over, I will never, ever, take refuge!

t_ch: 相信我，只要你前往主城区，你就会明白的。那里有许多如鲜花般绚烂的少女，正期许着我能够支撑起她们心灵的天空。
t_en: Trust me, you'll understand when you get to the town center. Many beautiful young ladies are expecting me to be the pillar of their souls.

t_ch: 一旦想到她们失望的眉眼，我的心比刀割还要刺痛。所以我不会，也绝不能前往避难。
t_en: It pains me whenever I think of the disappointment in their eyes. I will never take refuge.

t_ch: 你的每个动作和说话方式都好像精心设计过……
t_en: Your every movement and your ways of talking seem calculated...

t_ch: 我理解你的意思，之前也有很多人说过类似的话。
t_en: I understand what you mean. Many people have told me the same thing.

t_ch: 大家总之会被我的一些肢体语言或者是眼神所吸引注意，这一点我也十分困扰。
t_en: People always get attracted by my body language and my eyes. I find it really frustrating.

t_ch: 即便我努力克制，精心设计，希望不要为他人添麻烦，但也都是徒劳。
t_en: No matter how hard I try to refrain myself and avoid causing trouble for others, it never works.

t_ch: 或许这就是所说的，由内而外，无法控制吧？
t_en: Maybe it's just a part of me that's beyond my control.

t_ch: 你在这里真的不是在添乱吗？
t_en: Are you sure you are not causing trouble here?

t_ch: 希望你能够尊重我！我在这里的行为，不仅是为了我自己的灵魂誓言，更是为了众多生灵的平安！
t_en: Show me some respect, please! I do what I do, not only because I swore on my soul, but also for the sake of peace!

t_ch: 你可以不与我为伴，也可以不理解我的行动。但请你不要污蔑、践踏我的尊严！
t_en: I never asked you to join me or to understand my actions. But, please do not slander or trample on my dignity!

t_ch: 对你而言，我的语言或许是噪音；但对于真正的迷途之人而言，我的声音就是夜海的港灯！
t_en: My words may be noise to you, but for people who are lost, my voice is like a lighthouse in the stormy seas!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 无论如何，请你赶快前往避难。只有见到大家幸福的笑容，我才能安心。
t_en: Anyway, you should take refuge as soon as possible. I will be relieved when I see the smile on people's faces.

t_ch: 守护大家的心灵，坚守最后的港湾。这，就是我卡波特的职责。
t_en: Safeguard everyone's heart and defend our harbor. This is my job, Capote's job.

t_ch: 哼哼，搅动一潭死水最快的办法莫过于从外边投下一枚核弹。
t_en: Hmph, the quickest way to stir up a pond of stagnant water is to throw in a nuclear bomb from the outside.

t_ch: 就像…现在这样。
t_en: Like... What's happening now.

t_ch: 哦，你听见了？不用在意，只是无聊的自言自语。
t_en: Oh, did you hear that? Don't mind me. I was just mumbling nonsense to myself.

t_ch: 投下一颗石子就行了吧？！
t_en: Why don't you just throw a stone!?

t_ch: 呵，年轻人！
t_en: Hmph, young people!

t_ch: 一颗石子只能翻得起一朵水花，但一枚核弹却能引发一场海啸！
t_en: A stone can cause a splash, but a nuclear bomb can trigger a tsunami!

t_ch: 而聪明的人，就会借这海啸拉踩上位一气呵成。
t_en: Those who are smart will know how to take advantage of the tsunami and climb their way up the food chain.

t_ch: 新时代的弄潮儿伊丽莎白•沃森，正是在下。
t_en: I am Elizabeth Watson, the trendsetter of the new era.

t_ch: 我跟你很熟么？
t_en: Do we know each other?

t_ch: …不熟，但我们可以熟。我伊丽莎白•沃森一看就知道你不是泛泛之辈。
t_en: ...Not really, but we can. I, Elizabeth Watson, can tell right away you are no ordinary person.

t_ch: 只要你也跟我一样不甘平庸，我们就是异父异母的亲{F#姐妹}{M#姐弟}。
t_en: If you are like me and are unwilling to surrender to mediocrity, then we are siblings of a different mother.

t_ch: 这场袭击在别人眼里是一个灾难，但在聪明人眼里却是一场机遇。
t_en: This attack may seem like a disaster to many people, but it is an opportunity for those who are smart.

t_ch: 好好把握，姐妹。这里的一切，可没有你看上去那么简单。
t_en: Seize it, my brethren. Nothing here is as simple as it seems.

t_ch: 我还有些事情搞不懂…
t_en: There's something I can't wrap my head around...

t_ch: 那你可就找对人了，其实我正在调查这场袭击的内幕。
t_en: You've come to the right person. I am investigating this attack.

t_ch: 那你目前有什么发现？
t_en: What have you found so far?

t_ch: 凭我跟你现在的关系，我还不能把所有的消息都告诉你。
t_en: With our current relationship, I can't tell you everything I know.

t_ch: 但如果只是一些皮毛，那还可以说说。
t_en: But I can tell you something less important.

t_ch: 比如，这场袭击里有空间站的「某些科员」在搞事情。
t_en: For example, "some researchers" from the space station could be involved in this attack.

t_ch: 虽然我手里还没拿到决定性的证据，但时间一定会证明我是对的。
t_en: Although I haven't found any conclusive evidence yet, time will prove me right.

t_ch: 袭击者到底有什么目的？
t_en: What was the attacker's purpose?

t_ch: 年轻人的问题问得很犀利啊。
t_en: Kiddo, your question is very sharp.

t_ch: 任何的架构都是不完善的，稍有不慎随时都会有坍塌的风险。
t_en: No structure is perfect. There is always a risk of collapse at any time.

t_ch: 造成这场袭击的原因是多方面的：防护不力，内外勾结……
t_en: There are many reasons behind this attack: Collusion, lack of defensive power...

t_ch: 但这些都不重要！重要的是，袭击发生后的现在空间站会发生什么样的改变！
t_en: But none of this matters! The only thing that matters is the changes brought to the space station by this attack!

t_ch: 接下来你打算怎么做？
t_en: What are you going to do?

t_ch: 富贵险中求，当然是挖地三尺把那些家伙彻底揪出来。
t_en: High risk, high reward. I am going to dig deep and find those crooks.

t_ch: 放心，什么时候该冲，什么时候该逃，我心里有谱得很。
t_en: Don't worry about me, I know very well when I should act and when I should bolt.

t_ch: 倒是你的存在有些特殊且刻意…我闻到了阴谋的味道。嘘，不要打扰我思考。
t_en: If anything, your presence is somewhat peculiar and deliberate... I smell a conspiracy. Shh, don't interrupt my train of thought.

t_ch: 外面来的家伙…我闻到了阴谋的味道。嘘，不要打扰我思考。
t_en: Outsiders... I smell a conspiracy. Shh, don't interrupt my train of thought.

t_ch: 打扰了。
t_en: Sorry.

t_ch: 你…唉，现在的年轻人，一点耐心都没有。
t_en: You... *sigh* Young people these days have no patience at all.

t_ch: 给你个忠告，出门在外要学会保护自己，知人知面不知心……
t_en: Here's my advice for you. Learn to protect yourself when you are outside. You never know what people are thinking.

t_ch: 你挫败绝灭大君阴谋的事迹我已了然，拥有如此非凡天赋的新人可不多见，这是你应得的东西。希望你在未来的人生中，也可以保持当时的英勇。
t_en: I heard how you destroyed the schemes of a Lord Ravager. It is rare for a youngster to have such outstanding talents, and you've earned this reward. I hope you retain the courage you showed at that moment throughout your life in the future.

t_ch: 我最亲爱的仰慕者，你的信件我已经收到…你最珍重的唯一偶像，卡波特。一个字不许多，一个字不许少，以免让粉丝觉得差别待遇。封缄复信的火漆要用金银复色的蜡粒，再盖戳「波少」字样的章头。
t_en: My dearest admirer, your letter is well received... Your most cherished idol, Master Capote. Not one word more or one word less, or the fans might find it unfair. Gold and silver color wax should be used to seal the letters, and then stamped with the name Capote.

t_ch: 我最亲爱的仰慕者，你的信件我已经收到………
t_en: My dearest admirer, your letter is well received...

t_ch: 不好意思不好意思，我正在复习一些重要的工作规范，挡住您的路了吗？
t_en: I am sorry. I am in the middle of reviewing some important rules for my work. Am I in your way?

t_ch: 其实没有，不用抱歉。
t_en: No, you are not. Don't worry about it.

t_ch: 那就好，怪我太过全神贯注了，请您谅解。如果不这样，答错了卡波特大人的抽查，我可就有麻烦了……
t_en: That's good. I was so focused. Please forgive me. I will get in trouble if I fail to answer Master Capote's questions...

t_ch: 工作规范？
t_en: Rules?

t_ch: 嗯。作为卡波特大人的秘书，必须对他的日常行止负责。要是因为我的个人疏忽，损害了他的公共形象，我可就有麻烦了……
t_en: Yeah. As Master Capote's secretary, I must be responsible for his daily activities. If my personal negligence damages his public image, I will be in trouble...

t_ch: 还以为遇到了复读机。
t_en: I thought I bumped into a repeater.

t_ch: 复读也是没有办法的办法。都怪我之前没有随身携带纸笔，记录卡波特大人的吩咐。现在，只能依靠这不争气的脑袋了……
t_en: I have no choice. It was my fault I didn't bring a pen and paper with me to record Master Capote's orders. Now, I have to rely on my useless brain.

t_ch: …你最珍重的护法骑士，卡波特…封缄复信的火漆要用朱红色蜡粒，再盖戳「卡少」字样的章头……
t_en: ...Your most cherished hero, Capote... Red wax should be used to seal the letters, and then stamped with Capote...

t_ch: 那个…
t_en: Um...

t_ch: 嗯？您怎么欲言又止，不会是我记错了吧，应该不会啊…还是您也有什么事要交代我吗？
t_en: Yeah? Do you have something to say? Did I miss something again... Or do you have an order for me?

t_ch: 你刚才记的全错了。
t_en: You got it all wrong.

t_ch: 你别吓我啊？！这要是记错了，我的工作就会全面搁浅。
t_en: Don't scare me like that! If I get it wrong, I will lose my job.

t_ch: 不会吧……我再审查一遍好了…要是这次又出错，我肯定完蛋了……
t_en: I can't be... let me check again... If I make another mistake, I'll be dead...

t_ch: 需要帮忙吗？
t_en: Do you need help?

t_ch: 不用了不用了，卡波特大人强调过，他的私人事务不能随意经他人手。
t_en: No. Master Capote told me specifically that his personal affairs should not be handled by random strangers.

t_ch: 今天我的日程是，帮助卡波特大人「给粉丝回信」、「取回定制午餐」、「熨烫服装」、「给第二批粉丝回信」、「取回定制晚餐」、「给第三批粉丝回信」……
t_en: This is my schedule for the day: Help Master Capote reply to his fans, fetch his personalized lunch, iron clothes, reply to the second batch of his fans, fetch personalized dinner, reply to the third batch of his fans...

t_ch: 排得满满当当，喘口气的时间都快没了。这种时候，最忌讳忙中出错。
t_en: My schedule is so packed that I can't even spare time to breathe. I better not make any mistakes.

t_ch: 这些是你应该做的工作吗？
t_en: Are these supposed to be your job?

t_ch: 波少吩咐的工作，自然就是我应该做的工作啊。
t_en: Master Capote ordered me to do these things. Of course they are part of my job.

t_ch: 做秘书，最重要的就是完成方方面面的维护。工作的涵盖范围肯定就会广泛一些，边界也会相对模糊……
t_en: As his secretary, the most important thing is to keep track of everything. The scope of work can be large and the lines can be blurry sometimes...

t_ch: 虽然辛苦，但是多复习几次，肯定不会再出错了。
t_en: Although it's hard, I believe practice makes perfect.

t_ch: 我没什么要问了……
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 那我就继续了，如果我有打扰到你，请和我讲哦。
t_en: I should get back to work. Please let me know if I've interrupted you.

t_ch: 得小心，不能靠近那边……嗯……那里也不能去。
t_en: Better be careful, shouldn't go over there... well... not there either.

t_ch: 根据指南显示，这些地方都曾发现军团余孽……
t_en: According to the guide, remnants of the Legion have been spotted in these areas...

t_ch: 所以去那个地方的最安全路径……
t_en: So the safest way to go...

t_ch: 竟然要绕这么远的路吗？
t_en: Go all the way around?

t_ch: 你需要帮助吗？
t_en: Do you need help?

t_ch: 哦，不，不，我可以做到，一个人也可以。
t_en: Oh, no, it's fine. I can do it on my own.

t_ch: 只是提交一些文件，没有什么问题的……多走一些路罢了。
t_en: I just need to submit some documents. I'll be fine... I just need to walk a bit further.

t_ch: 别怪我没提醒你，军团余孽尚未清缴完毕，现在的空间站还是危机四伏。
t_en: Don't say I didn't warn you. The station hasn't been cleared of Remnants of the Legion yet, there's still danger lurking around every corner.

t_ch: 为了你自己的安全，最好还是少去人少的地方晃悠吧！
t_en: For your own safety, you'd better not go to remote places!

t_ch: 你看上去很焦虑。
t_en: You look anxious.

t_ch: 没有人能做到在经历那样一场灾难之后，还保持心情愉悦吧？
t_en: After such a disaster, who can be happy? Am I right?

t_ch: 只要一闭上眼睛，我就感觉那些身姿怪异的敌人又会出现在我眼前……
t_en: Whenever I close my eyes, I can feel those twisted enemies appear in front of me...

t_ch: 这怎么能叫人不担心，不害怕呢！
t_en: How can I not be worried? How can I not be scared?

t_ch: 还有一些事情想要问…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 你为什么不离开这里？
t_en: Why didn't you leave?

t_ch: 离开这里，又有什么地方是绝对安全的呢？稍微想一想就能知道答案。
t_en: Where can I find absolute safety if I leave? It's a no-brainer.

t_ch: 答案就是——无论身在何处，都没有绝对的安全！
t_en: The answer is, no matter where you go, no place is absolutely safe!

t_ch: 更何况，寰宇之间，应该很难有比这里更加安全的地方了吧？
t_en: Besides, I don't think there's any other place in the universe safer than here.

t_ch: 哎，说到底，待在这里，也是无奈啊……
t_en: Sigh. At the end of the day, I stayed because I don't really have a choice...

t_ch: 这次入侵对你造成了什么影响？
t_en: How did the invasion affect your life?

t_ch: 我其实很多天没有睡过一次好觉了。
t_en: In fact, I haven't had a good sleep for many days.

t_ch: 而且……我总是觉得，身边会突然出现一道裂缝。而那些怪物，就会从裂缝里缓缓走出来……
t_en: And... I always feel like a crack might suddenly appear next to me. And those monsters will slowly emerge out of the crack...

t_ch: 啊，这一切真的是太可怕了！我不知道要过多久，才能彻底遗忘！
t_en: Ahhh, this is really terrible! I don't know how long it will take before I can get this out of my head!

t_ch: 对于复兴建设，你有没有什么想法？
t_en: What's your take on the recovery construction?

t_ch: 我记得艾丝妲站长已经安排了相关部门在进行专门的复兴与安全区开辟吧？
t_en: I remember Lead Researcher Asta has organized the relevant departments to work on the recovery and establishment of safe zones, right?

t_ch: 不过……除了展望未来进行复兴，还有很多需要完成的事情吧？
t_en: However... Other than looking towards the future, there is still a lot to be done.

t_ch: 那些在这次灾难中失去生命的人……甚至连铭牌都没能被完整保存……
t_en: People who've lost their lives in this disaster... Some of them didn't even have an intact nameplate left behind...

t_ch: 那他们又该有谁去铭记，谁去哀悼呢？
t_en: Who will remember them? And who is going to mourn them?

t_ch: 我没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 哎，总之，还是要注意安全。现在的情况，发生什么意外都不足为奇……
t_en: In short, please be careful. The way things are right now, anything can happen.

t_ch: 哎，生命无常，生死难料。说不定这一次见面，就是我们最后一次呢？
t_en: Sigh. Life is unpredictable. Who knows if this will be the last time we meet?

t_ch: 抬起你们骄傲的头颅吧！不要再自欺欺人地忽视那些就在你们眼皮子底下的惨状！
t_en: Raise your proud heads! Don't try to deceive yourself by ignoring the tragedies right in front of your eyes!

t_ch: 当你肆意使唤机械臂为你，只为给你制作一杯手打一千次拿铁的时候，有没有想过它的感受？
t_en: Have you ever thought about the feeling of the robot arm when you order a cup of latte that requires <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> wisks?

t_ch: 当你对那些非人奇物施以过量外部刺激，只为看一看它的特有反应的时候，有没有想过它也会痛苦？
t_en: Did it ever occur to you that the non-human Curios can also feel pain when you cast excessive external stimulation to it just to see its unique response?

t_ch: 情感，不只是人类才有的专有名词。正视身边非人生物的惨状，才是真正理解爱的第一步！
t_en: Emotion is not exclusive to humans. Facing the tragedy of non-human creatures around you is the first step to truly understand love!

t_ch: 如何保障非人生物权益？
t_en: How can you protect the rights and interests of non-human creatures?

t_ch: 哦！太好了！在我大声喧嚷了一天之后，总算遇到了一位明眼人！
t_en: Oh, great! After yelling and shouting for a whole day, there's finally someone who understands me!

t_ch: 我的兄弟，我的同胞。请允许我做一下自我介绍。
t_en: My brethern, my compatriot, please allow me to introduce myself.

t_ch: 我叫卢卡奇，目前是拟态保护协会会长、法政科科员。
t_en: My name is Lukacs. I am currently the president of the Mimicry Protection Association and a researcher at the Department of Galactic Geopolitics.

t_ch: 对于非人生物权益保障，这个议题非常宏观，如果您时间匆忙，我只能简述一二。
t_en: The protection of non-human creatures' rights and interests is a rather macro topic. If you are in a hurry, I can only give you a brief introduction.

t_ch: 最简单的方面——上次的军团入侵。人们都慌不择路，却没有人去关心那些奇物的安危！
t_en: Let's start from the most simple aspect — the last Legion invasion. People were desperate to escape but no one cared about the safety of the Curios!

t_ch: 这就是如今空间站的现状，也是非人生物权益不得保障的重要体现！
t_en: This is the space station's current situation. It's also a representation that the non-human creatures' rights are not protected!

t_ch: 如何缓解机器人被奴役现状？
t_en: How can we alleviate the enslavement of robots?

t_ch: 问到了点子上，我的朋友。
t_en: You hit the nail on the head, my friend.

t_ch: 不难看出你果真有一双慧眼，善于发现问题最本质的痛点所在。
t_en: I can see you have sharp eyes and you are good at spotting the most essential pain point of the issue.

t_ch: 空间站的科技水平想必大家有目共睹，可是大家却丝毫没有对于机械抱有敬畏之心。
t_en: I'm sure everyone can see the level of science and technology in the space station. However, people show no respect for machines.

t_ch: 用坏了就扔，不好用了就换，这样的思维模式是非常危险的。
t_en: People get rid of them when broken, and replace them when they start to slow down. It's a very dangerous mindset.

t_ch: 这就是当下的情况！非常糟糕！
t_en: This is the current situation! It is very bad!

t_ch: 我还有一些问题想要问…
t_en: I still got some questions for you...

t_ch: 尽管问吧，我的伙伴。我一定知无不言言无不尽，解答你心中所有的疑惑。
t_en: Any questions you have, my friend. I will tell you everything I know to solve all the questions in your heart.

t_ch: 为什么会想到保障非人生物权益？
t_en: Why do you want to protect the rights and interests of non-human creatures?

t_ch: 问得很好！只有理清了行动的本真理念，才会达成真正的互相理解。
t_en: Good question! Only by understanding the motives behind the actions can mutual understanding be truly achieved.

t_ch: 在空间站的无数奇物藏品中，生命其实不止存在于人类这个载体。
t_en: Among the countless Curio collections of the space station, life does not only exist in the human receptacle.

t_ch: 它们经常会栖息于一些人类想不到的地方——比如一张桌子，或者一个杯子。
t_en: They often inhabit places that humans can't imagine — such as a table or a cup.

t_ch: 然而，仅仅是因为它们的外形，并不为人类所普遍认识，它们的生命就不被重视了！
t_en: However, just because their appearances are not understood by humans, their lives are not valued!

t_ch: 何等荒谬？生命岂能因为外显的形式不同，就出现高低贵贱之分呢？
t_en: How ridiculous? How can anyone segregate life into different levels just because of their external forms?

t_ch: 正因如此，我决定成立拟态保护协会，去保障非人生物的各项权益。
t_en: For this very reason, I decided to establish the Mimicry Protection Association to protect the rights and interests of non-human creatures.

t_ch: 作为拟态保护协会会长，你做过些什么？
t_en: What have you done as the president of the Mimicry Protection Association?

t_ch: 我势单力薄，影响力或许会让你失望。
t_en: I am powerless on my own. My influence may disappoint you.

t_ch: 但是我敢用自己的灵魂发誓，我从未在这件事上有所懈怠。每一天，我都在为了推广拟态保护协会而努力！
t_en: But I swear on my soul that I never treated this matter with any less importance. Every day, I work hard to promote the Mimicry Protection Association!

t_ch: 除了像是现在这样招募志同道合之士外，我还在闲暇之余撰写了《废除机器人奴役法案》。虽然还是草稿，但是框架已经初步成型。
t_en: Other than recruiting like-minded people like now, I also compiled the Robot Slavery Abolition Act. Although it is only a draft, I've got the preliminary framework set up.

t_ch: 相信未来，我们一定可以改变这个冰冷的现状！
t_en: Have some faith in the future. I'm sure we can change this cold-blooded status quo!

t_ch: 空间站其他人对于你的行为怎么看待？
t_en: What are the other people's perspectives on your actions?

t_ch: 很遗憾，这里的科员大多数是一些自私之辈。他们过分关注眼前短浅的利益，升职加薪一类的事情更值得他们去付出心血。
t_en: Unfortunately, most of the researchers here are selfish. They care too much about short-term interests. They are more willing to put their efforts into things like promotion and salary.

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我也老实说吧。他们认为我的想法过于天真烂漫、不切实际。
t_en: I'll be honest with you. They think my idea is premature and unrealistic.

t_ch: 但好在偶尔会有几个同你一样的知音出现，让我有信心坚持在这一条正确的道路上。
t_en: But fortunately, once in a while I meet people like you who share the ambition. It gives me faith to stick to the right path.

t_ch: 没有什么问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 虽然交流时间不长，但我能感受到，你已经领悟了我想要表达的所有含义。
t_en: Although we didn't chat long, I can feel that you already understood everything I try to express.

t_ch: 希望以后我们可以为了非人生物的权益，做出更多的努力！
t_en: I hope we can make more efforts in protecting the rights and interests of non-human creatures in the future!

t_ch: 真不知道她在那边过得怎么样……
t_en: I wonder how she's doing over there...

t_ch: 哎，我这是今天第几次忍不住想她了？
t_en: Sigh. I already lost count of how many times I've thought about her today.

t_ch: 一闭上眼睛，脑子里出现的都是她的容貌……这下子还怎么能好好工作啊。
t_en: Whenever I close my eyes, all I can think of is her face... How am I supposed to work?

t_ch: 你在思念谁？
t_en: Who are you thinking about?

t_ch: 哦！你吓了我一跳！我还以为是隔壁科室的混蛋又来找麻烦了！
t_en: Hey! You scared me! I thought it was the scoundrels from the department next door looking for trouble here!

t_ch: 看你的样子，以前没见过？是新来空间站的科员吗？
t_en: I don't think I've seen you before. Are you a new researcher on the space station?

t_ch: 我叫洛奇，是地概科的科员。
t_en: My name is Rocky. I'm a researcher from the Department of Cosmic Geography.

t_ch: 关于我想念的人……她叫莱斯莉。你可能没有听说过她的传说。
t_en: Who am I thinking about... Her name is Lesley. You may not have heard of the legends about her.

t_ch: 总之，你在空间站待得越久，以后一定会认识她的。
t_en: Anyway, if you stay long enough on the space station, I'm sure you'll get to know her in the future.

t_ch: 她不在空间站吗？
t_en: Is she not on the space station?

t_ch: 她作为密卷科的明星科员，作为访问学者出使了丰饶星域。
t_en: As a celebrity researcher of the Department of Insight, she is on a visit to the Abundance segmentum.

t_ch: 幸好科技发展速度很快，我和她虽然见不到面，但可以用其他方式来往。
t_en: Thanks to technological development, although we don't get to see each other, we can communicate via other ways.

t_ch: 每次我想她的时候，我就会把她的容貌雕刻在这些石头上——你看！
t_en: Every time I think about her, I carve her face on a rock. Look at these rocks!

t_ch: 不过我手艺不好，雕刻出来的样子和莱斯莉本人差距很大。她可比这些石头好看多了！
t_en: But my crafting skill is not very good. The face I carve on the rocks doesn't really look like her. She looks much better than these!

t_ch: 还有些事想问你…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 莱斯莉多久会回来呢？
t_en: When will Lesley come back?

t_ch: 这个……我也说不准。毕竟本琥珀纪，只有她一个人有幸前往丰饶星域。
t_en: Well... I'm not sure. After all, she's the only lucky one who got to visit the Abundance Segmentum in this Amber Era.

t_ch: 不过在那个地方，她每日沐浴神眷，与优秀的同事一起完成科考，一定比在空间站要更加幸福开心。
t_en: However, she is bathed in divine blessings every day over there. She must be much happier doing research with other outstanding researchers over there than staying on the space station.

t_ch: 既然对她而言，是幸福的体验，那时间长一些，也是一种享受吧？
t_en: Since it is such a happy experience for her, it must be enjoyable to stay there for a longer time, right?

t_ch: 你喜欢莱斯莉吗？
t_en: Do you like Lesley?

t_ch: 这…这不是明摆着的嘛！她就是我认定的那个女孩了……
t_en: Isn't it... Obvious? She is the one!

t_ch: 只可惜，我还没有正式完成对她的告白。
t_en: Unfortunately, I haven't officially confessed my love for her.

t_ch: 不过也好，这样我就有更多的时间去追求她，并等待她的回应啦！
t_en: It's good though. That way I can have more time to pursue her and wait for her response!

t_ch: 隔壁科室的混蛋是谁？
t_en: Who are the scoundrels from the department next door?

t_ch: 他们啊？一帮来找麻烦的流氓而已！
t_en: Those people? They are just a bunch of hooligans looking for trouble!

t_ch: 科室与科室之间本就研究方法不同，有些时候发生学术冲突很正常。但总是有人喜欢对人不对事，阴阳怪气不说还跑过来欺负人。
t_en: Different departments adopt different research methods. Academic conflict is normal. But some people just take things too personally and come harass other people.

t_ch: 所以我经常会教训教训那帮臭小子！看他们还敢不敢再来找地概科室的麻烦！
t_en: That's why I often teach them a good lesson! I'll see if they still dare to look for troubles at the Department of Cosmic Geography!

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 倒也不打扰。来个人和我说说话，换换心思也好。
t_en: You didn't bother me. It's good to have someone talk to me and distract me.

t_ch: 毕竟，总不能一直惦记着莱斯莉，对吧？
t_en: After all, I shouldn't keep thinking about Lesley, should I?

t_ch: 第九杯…加油，埃美丽。这不算什么，只要捏着鼻子就能喝下去。
t_en: The ninth cup... Come on, Emily. This is nothing. Just pinch your nose and drink it.

t_ch: 没错，捏住鼻子，这玩意儿不过是褐色的液体而已。
t_en: Yes, pinch your nose. It's just brown liquid.

t_ch: 咳……咳咳！！好难受…这也太难喝了。
t_en: *Cough* I can't take it anymore... it's disgusting.

t_ch: 为什么要喝这么多咖啡？
t_en: Why are you drinking so much coffee?

t_ch: 你是谁？我怎么没有见过你？
t_en: Who are you? How come I've never seen you?

t_ch: 闲杂人等别管闲事，我喝不喝咖啡和你没有任何关系！
t_en: Mind your own business. Whether I drink coffee or not has nothing to do with you!

t_ch: 你看上去并不喜欢喝咖啡。
t_en: You don't seem to enjoy drinking coffee.

t_ch: 关你什么事？不爱喝咖啡就不可以喝咖啡了吗？没有这样的规定吧？
t_en: It's none of your business! Who says I can't drink coffee if I don't like it? Is this against the law?

t_ch: 说到底，咖啡就是功能性饮料的一种，就像是人饿了会吃饭，人困倦了就会喝咖啡！
t_en: At the end of the day, coffee is just a functional beverage. People eat when they are hungry, and people drink coffee when they feel tired!

t_ch: 我有些事情想问你…
t_en: I have a few questions for you...

t_ch: 你是想要打破喝咖啡的记录吗？
t_en: Are you trying to break the record for drinking coffee?

t_ch: 你…你怎么知道？难道说你已经听说了那个女人的故事？
t_en: How... How did you know that? Have you heard about the story of that woman?

t_ch: 也难怪，种子岛那边的人嘴巴一天到晚叭叭不停，动不动就吹嘘什么「莱斯莉的十一杯畅饮咖啡」神话。
t_en: Why am I surprised? People on Seed Isle never shut up. They keep bragging about the legend of Lesley's <unbreak>11</unbreak> Cups of Coffee.

t_ch: 她莱斯莉能做到的，我埃美丽也能做到，而且会做的比她更强、更好！
t_en: Whatever Lesley can do, I can do it too. And I will do it even better!

t_ch: 关于莱斯莉。
t_en: About Lesley.

t_ch: 我是那位年轻有位的星神学者莱斯莉的妹妹，目前在密卷科担任科员，我叫埃美丽。
t_en: I'm the younger sister of Lesley the prodigy Aeon scholar. I am Emily. I currently work as a researcher at the Department of Insight.

t_ch: 请你搞清楚，我是埃美丽！虽然从血亲上说我是她的妹妹，但希望你可以独立地认识我，不要和其他人一样，老是把我和那个女人扯上关系。
t_en: Understand this, I'm Emily! Even though technically I'm her sister, I hope you can get to know me as an independent individual, instead of associating me with that woman, like everybody else.

t_ch: 我只不过比她晚出生一些日子，但无论是才学还是实践，我都比她强。
t_en: I was only born a bit later than her. I am better than her no matter in talent or practice.

t_ch: 只要能给我一个相同的机会，我一定也能在空间站留下属于我的传说……
t_en: As long as I am given the same opportunity, I'm sure I will be able to create my own legend on the space station...

t_ch: 关于莱斯莉的传闻。
t_en: About rumors on Lesley.

t_ch: 咖啡畅饮？科研天才？这种传闻空间站到处都是，你干嘛非得来找我问？故意的是吧！
t_en: Coffee consumption? Research genius? These rumors are everywhere on the space station. Why do you have to come to me to ask? Are you trying to rile me up?

t_ch: 如果你是想来求证那些传闻的真假，我可以很肯定地告诉你：没错，莱斯莉就是那样的一个天才。
t_en: If you're here to verify the validity of the rumors, I can tell you with <unbreak>100%</unbreak> certainty that yes, Lesley is just a genius like that.

t_ch: 但如果你是想通过我，得知什么小道消息，或者是打一些歪主意…我可警告你，最好把那些心思收一收！
t_en: But if you're thinking of getting any insider information or playing some weird games through me... I'm warning you, it's best you drop those thoughts right now!

t_ch: 到处打听关于她的事情，多半没什么好心思。你最好注意些，别让我发现你的阴谋诡计。
t_en: What good intention could you possibly have by asking around about her? You better watch out. Don't let me find out your scheme.

t_ch: 没什么需要继续问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 算你识相。以后少来管教我，也不要到处打听关于莱斯莉的消息。
t_en: That's a wise thing to do. Leave me alone. And stop prying on Lesley.

t_ch: 真是困扰，这次送波少这个好，还是那个好呢……
t_en: What a headache. Which one should I give to Master Capote...

t_ch: 唔，要不干脆把这两个奇物都送给波少吧？这样就不用担心他不喜欢了~
t_en: Hmm, why don't I give both Curios to him? That way I don't have to worry that he might not like it.

t_ch: 诶，要是两个都不喜欢，岂不是双重踩雷，波少会讨厌我的吧……
t_en: But if he dislikes both of them, wouldn't it be twice as bad? Master Capote is going to hate me...

t_ch: 哎，到底该怎么办才好啊。
t_en: Ahhh, what should I do?

t_ch: 你有权限赠送奇物？
t_en: Do you have the right to give Curios as a gift?

t_ch: 啊！你是谁？怎么突然出现在我背后？难道是故意偷听世玲说话？！
t_en: Ah! Who are you? How did you suddenly show up behind me? Were you eavesdropping on me!?

t_ch: 看你的穿着不属于任何一个科室…呼，放心了。
t_en: You don't look like you belong to any department... Phew, I'm relieved.

t_ch: 我是应物科的科员温世玲，这些奇物……是我根据规定，需要交给波少的。
t_en: I'm Wen Shiling, a researcher from the Department of Implement Arts. According to the regulations... I'm supposed to give these Curios to Master Capote.

t_ch: 谁是波少？
t_en: Who is Master Capote?

t_ch: 你连波少都不认识？拜托，你不会今天才到空间站吧。还是说之前军团入侵的时候，把你脑袋伤到，导致失忆了？
t_en: What? You don't know Master Capote? You didn't only arrive today, did you? Or did the Legion break your head during their invasion and gave you amnesia?

t_ch: 算了算了，就让世玲再给你做一次关于波少的科普介绍吧。
t_en: Fine fine. Let me reintroduce Master Capote to you.

t_ch: 倘若要问空间站谁是最绚烂的星，那除黑塔大人之下，必然有卡波特大人的身影。
t_en: If you asked who is the most dazzling star on the space station other than Madam Herta, it would definitely be Master Capote.

t_ch: 他温和谦逊，又成熟冷静。优秀的内在性格促使他拥有了一副完美的外表……啊！这就是相由心生的含义吧！
t_en: He is gentle and modest, mature and composed. His inner excellence gives him a perfect appearance... Ahhhh! One's appearance is shaped by one's mind!

t_ch: 不过你可别打波少的主意！我可是空间站的头号波少粉丝！
t_en: But don't you dare to do anything to Master Capote! I am his number-one fan on the space station!

t_ch: 我还有些问题想问你…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 波少一般喜欢什么类型的奇物？
t_en: What kind of Curio does Master Capote usually prefer?

t_ch: 你这可算是问对人了。能够解答这个问题的，全空间站恐怕只有我世玲一人！
t_en: You're asking the right person. I've gotta be the only person in the space station who can answer this question!

t_ch: 对于波少的爱好，我可是了如指掌。就连他喝咖啡只加三块半的糖，我都铭记于心底。
t_en: I know Master Capote's hobbies like the back of my hand. I even remember he adds three and a half cubes of sugar to his coffee.

t_ch: 至于奇物……咳咳，你可别想套世玲的话！我都说过了，那些奇物是根据规定分发的，不会按照科员的喜好来！
t_en: As for the Curios... Ehem, you think you can fool me into answering this? As I said, the Curios are distributed according to regulations, instead of the researcher's preferences!

t_ch: 应物科都是按照什么规矩分发奇物？
t_en: What is the Department of Implement Arts' regulations in terms of distributing Curios?

t_ch: 这……这关你什么事！空间站有空间站的规矩，应物科也有应物科的准则。
t_en: It's... it's none of your business! The space station has its own rules and regulations, so does our department.

t_ch: 我给波少分发的奇物，那，那可都是走正规流程的。你别多想啊。
t_en: The Curios I distribute to Master Capote are through the normal procedure. Don't think too much about it.

t_ch: 不过，不过应物科工作繁忙，你就算不相信，也别去打听了。省得给我们添麻烦。
t_en: Anyway, the Department of Implement Arts is very busy. Don't ask around even if you don't believe me. Save us some troubles.

t_ch: 哎呀，世玲真是最讨厌你这种刨根问底的新人啦！
t_en: Jeez, I hate newbies like you who always try to get to the bottom of everything!

t_ch: 你也给我整一个奇物呗。
t_en: Give me a Curio, too.

t_ch: 哈！？你知不知道你在说什么话？奇物怎么可能是想要就能给的！
t_en: Huh!? Do you even know what you're talking about? I can't just give a Curio to a random person!

t_ch: 除了波少，谁还可以不走流程就拿到奇物？谁还可以自行挑选奇物？你以为你这样初来乍到的新人，就能拥有波少的学识和水平吗？
t_en: Except for Master Capote, no one gets Curios without going through the normal process. No one gets to choose Curios. You think a newbie like you can match Master Capote's knowledge and standard?

t_ch: 咳咳，世玲的意思是，你的科员等级过低，目前无法申请奇物作为研究。其他的事，你就少打听啦！
t_en: Ehem, I mean, your researcher level is too low. You can't apply for a Curio for your research at the moment. Anything else is none of your business!

t_ch: 我没什么要问的了。
t_en: That's all I have to ask.

t_ch: 哼，总算是问完了。亏世玲还大费周章地给你科普空间站的常识，没想到你倒是想知道更多不该知道的东西！
t_en: Phew, finally. I spent so much effort introducing the basics of the space station to you, but you're interested in things you shouldn't know!

t_ch: 你这都卖些什么？
t_en: What have you got for sale here, then?

t_ch: 让我检查一遍所有的流程是否到位：研究课题，没有推进；研究进度汇报会，被临时取消；绩效考核评估标准，等待重新拟定……
t_en: Let me double-check everything: Research topic, no progress; research progress report meeting, canceled last minute; performance review criteria, under evaluation...

t_ch: 很好！一切都井然有序！现在就是最佳的科研时光~
t_en: Good! Everything's in place! Now, this is the best time for scientific research.

t_ch: 嗯哼…我好像没见过你？不过有缘千里来相会，尤其是忙里偷闲这种难得的悠哉时光。
t_en: Uh-huh... I don't think I've seen you before. Nevertheless, it seems to be our fate to meet each other like this, especially during the leisure time away from work.

t_ch: 我看你也不是很忙的样子。
t_en: You don't look very busy.

t_ch: 此言差矣。忙碌与否不是看手头上有没有正在进行的工作，而是在于头脑里是否在发生急剧的风暴。这就是我温世齐的工作哲学。
t_en: That's not true. How busy I am should not be judged by any existing projects, but whether any brainstorming is happening in my head. This is my work philosophy.

t_ch: 我虽然看上去此时此刻无所事事，但其实我的内心非常关注军团来袭之后，咱们空间站的后续复兴工作！
t_en: Although I may seem pretty leisurely, deep down I do care a lot about the space station's restoration after the Legion invasion!

t_ch: 你看我的眉毛是不是拧在了一起？这就是我心系空间站的表现啊。
t_en: Have you noticed how intertwined my eyebrows are right now? That's how much I care about the space station.

t_ch: 你不用工作的吗？
t_en: Don't you have work to do?

t_ch: 工作的定义是什么？是坐在实验桌前为了一个模糊的数据投入无尽的时间？还是为了一份绩效考核不断思考该如何完成一场非凡的演讲？
t_en: What should be counted as work? Is it sitting behind a desk all day, devoting all your life to some random pieces of ill-defined data? Or racking your brain, trying to come up with a way to deliver convincing arguments for the sake of a performance review?

t_ch: 表面上的行动不能说明什么，如果工作中的心不是工作的状态，那么和走神又有什么区别？
t_en: Outward appearance doesn't say anything about your work attitude. If your mind is not in work mode, how is it any different from slacking off at work?

t_ch: 同理，如果在休息的时候心系工作，那么这才叫真正的用心工作。你明白了吗？
t_en: Similarly, thinking about work during your break is what should be considered as true devotion to your work. You got it?

t_ch: 我还有一些事情想要问你…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 我看你好像是「公司」的人？
t_en: You look like someone from the IPC.

t_ch: 「公司」？我再说一遍，我是应物科的II级科员！II级！懂不懂II级科员的含金量？我现在只是兼顾着空间站员工内购系统的事务。
t_en: IPC? Let me remind you, I'm a Lv. 2 researcher at the Department of Implement Arts, okay? Level 2! Can you even comprehend how much a Lv. 2 researcher is worth? I'm just handling the internal purchase system for space station staff.

t_ch: 没办法，能者多劳嘛，我在应物科出了名的人缘好，所以大家就把和「公司」对接的重任交给了我！
t_en: Can't help it. No rest for the capable. I'm notoriously popular around the Department of Implement Arts, so everyone leaves the important job of liaising with the IPC over to me!

t_ch: 你看，绩效考核会推迟，这就意味着我不用熬夜去思考怎么做汇报了。看你的样子像是新来的，肯定不知道咱们这里的汇报流程有多烦琐……
t_en: Look, the performance review is postponed, which means I don't have to work late thinking about the report. You look like a newbie. You've no idea how cumbersome our reporting process is...

t_ch: 不仅如此，研究课题进度汇报也暂停了。这句话意味着什么，不用我多说了吧？
t_en: Moreover, the research progress report is also suspended. You don't need me to spell it out for you, do you?

t_ch: 综上所述，这场入侵可谓是一把双刃剑！不然我哪儿有时间像这样放松自己，感受生活啊？
t_en: To sum up, this invasion is a double-edged sword! Otherwise, I wouldn't have time to relax and enjoy my life!

t_ch: 「员工内购系统」是什么？
t_en: Internal purchase system? What's that?

t_ch: 你算是问对了人，我就是负责这个的。顾名思义，就是「公司」代理的购物系统，只对空间站的员工开放。
t_en: You've come to the right place. That's my line of work. Just as the name suggests, it's a purchase system subcontracted to the IPC that's only available for space station staff.

t_ch: 里面都是些有意思的东西，你要是想搞科研，多半用得着。只可惜…目前只接受「黑塔币」付款。
t_en: There's some pretty cool and useful stuff in there, especially if you're doing research. It's just... The only currency it accepts at the moment is Hertareum.

t_ch: 价格嘛，独家代理的，你懂的…整套系统就是为了回收咱们手里的「黑塔币」而设计的。当然，这话你可不能跟其他人说。
t_en: And it's an exclusive supply, if you get my drift... The whole thing is designed to recycle Hertareum. You can't actually say that to anyone, though.

t_ch: 你怎么一副不感兴趣的样子？难道这还不算有意思的事情么？
t_en: You don't seem very interested. Isn't it interesting to you?

t_ch: 你平时都做什么类型的课题研究？
t_en: What kind of research do you usually do?

t_ch: 拜托，可不可以不要在休息的时候讲工作？
t_en: Oh, come on, can you please not talk about work while I'm taking a break?

t_ch: 什么，你说现在也是工作时间？你这人还真是意料之外的死板。工作时间与非工作时间，难道是被时间表框定的吗？你这样的人很容易在三十岁前发际线就跑到头顶上去的。
t_en: What? Are you saying this is working hours too? You are surprisingly stubborn. Do you think working hours and non-working hours are specified by a timetable? At this rate, your hairline will recede before you turn <unbreak>30</unbreak>.

t_ch: 想聊课题，你随便抓个科员就行了。这种小事就不用来打扰我的休憩快乐了。
t_en: If all you want is to talk about research, just grab any researcher. Don't ruin my joy of slacking off.

t_ch: 我没有什么问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 闲聊也是一种休息的方式，如果你有兴趣可以再来找我——只要是和课题研究无关，我都会很乐意聊聊的。
t_en: Chatting is also a way of resting. Come chat with me if you are interested. I'll be very happy to talk about anything as long as it's not about research.

t_ch: 哈——哈欠……
t_en: *Yawning*

t_ch: 真困啊，难道这就是所谓的倦怠时间么…？干脆睡一会儿吧。
t_en: I'm so sleepy. Is this the so-called burnout time? I better go back to sleep.

t_ch: 不对，我好像还有什么事情要做来着……
t_en: Hold on, I think I've got something to do...

t_ch: 什么事呢……
t_en: What is it...

t_ch: 你手里拿着的是？
t_en: What are you holding?

t_ch: …特效药？
t_en: ...Wonder medicine?

t_ch: 哦，好像确实有个科室反应，说是科员精神状况突发问题，让我过去看看来着。
t_en: Oh, a department informed me that one of their researchers had an urgent psychological issue. They sent me to have a look.

t_ch: 好麻烦，这种事情让机器人过来取一下镇定剂不就好了么。
t_en: What a pain in the neck. Why don't they just send a robot to fetch a tranquilizer?

t_ch: 你口袋里装着的是？
t_en: What's in your pocket?

t_ch: …注射剂？
t_en: ...An injection?

t_ch: 让我想想，好像是听说有位患者的药剂不足，需要补充来着。
t_en: Let me see. I heard a patient ran out of medicine and needed supplies.

t_ch: 不过是哪位患者呢…啊，好像忘记了。
t_en: But which patient was it for...? Ahh, I forgot.

t_ch: 啧，又得回去看看记录，好麻烦。
t_en: Hmph. I have to go back and check my record again. What a pain in the neck.

t_ch: 我有一些问题想要问你…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 你是医生么？
t_en: Are you a doctor?

t_ch: 我是医疗科的成员乔安。具体负责的工作…或许比医生还杂乱一些。
t_en: I'm Joanne, a member of the Medical Department... My scope of work... may be even more complicated than the doctors'.

t_ch: 有什么身体不舒服的问题可别直接问我，我只负责工作分内的患者，临时偶遇的概不负责。
t_en: Don't come to me if you are not feeling well. I'm only responsible for the patients under my scope of work. I don't take care of any random people I bump into.

t_ch: 你看上去很困。
t_en: You look sleepy.

t_ch: 既然知道就别跟我搭话。打扰一个想打瞌睡的人，比打扰一个正在熟睡的人更加恶劣。
t_en: I'm glad you noticed. Stop talking to me then. Disturbing a person who is trying to sleep is even worse than waking up someone who's already asleep.

t_ch: 喂，你这幅不信任又担心的表情是怎么回事？
t_en: Hey, what's that distrustful and concerned look on your face?

t_ch: 我虽然很容易犯困，但工作的时候不会马虎的！你这家伙，该不会把我当成那种会把止血棉落在患者身体里的那种糊涂医生了吧？！
t_en: Although I easily feel sleepy at work, I take my job very seriously! Don't think of me as some stupid doctor who would leave hemostatic cotton inside their patient!

t_ch: 你看上去很忙。
t_en: You look busy.

t_ch: 既然你能察觉到，那就最好不要来烦我。
t_en: I'm glad you noticed. Then leave me alone.

t_ch: 不是送药就是补药，这种事情分明就该交给机器人吧？
t_en: I am either delivering or going to deliver medicine. They should've assigned this job to a robot, right?

t_ch: 要不是那什么物质军团来捣乱，这种事情才不会交到我手里。
t_en: If it wasn't for the Legion's invasion, this kind of thing wouldn't have become my duty.

t_ch: 真希望站长可以快点修复机器人配送系统啊……
t_en: I wish the lead researcher can fix the robot distribution system as soon as possible.

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I better leave you alone.

t_ch: 如果有身体不适，千万别去医疗科指名我，我每天可忙着呢。
t_en: If you are not feeling well, do not ask for me in the Medical Department. I am very busy.

t_ch: 哎，要这时候来口酒，最好再来点下酒菜……
t_en: Imagine if I have a bottle of wine and some snacks just about now...

t_ch: 还得再有个一块儿喝酒扯闲篇的人一块儿！嘿，那才叫过日子！
t_en: And someone to drink and chat with! Ha! C'est la vie!

t_ch: 小年轻，你好像是个生面孔啊？要不要来和我聊聊？
t_en: Kiddo, you don't look like you are from here? You wanna chat with me?

t_ch: 我……我不会喝酒。
t_en: I... I don't drink.

t_ch: 见了外了。这酒就和水没区别，咕咚咕咚从嘴里流到胃里，一下子全身热乎了就对了。其实也就比喝热水要辣喉咙点，没区别的。
t_en: No worries. Wine is no different from water. It flows from your mouth to your stomach and then your whole body warms up. It's just slightly spicier than hot water. They are nearly the same.

t_ch: 你这种刚来空间站的新人，很多时候做事儿是糊涂的。多和我温天翁聊聊，也算是借鉴一点老人的经验嘛！
t_en: As a newcomer on the space station, it's normal that you'll make mistakes sometimes. Chat with me to gain some experience from an old fellow!

t_ch: 你想要扯什么闲篇？
t_en: What do you want to chat about?

t_ch: 这年轻人，爱问问题是好事。我老温没啥本事，学术这方面还得看你自个儿造化。但别的事情嘛……我可有的说道咯！
t_en: Youngster, it's a good thing to ask questions. I'm not much to write home about. You can only count on yourself in terms of academics, but I have a lot to tell you about other things!

t_ch: 你可能认识的人还不多，有些故事跟你讲了，你连名字和脸都对不上号，听着没意思。如果你认识的人多，我可就能和你好好讲讲咯。
t_en: You may not know many people here. If I tell you a story where you can't match the names with the actual people, it won't be very interesting to you. If you know more people, I can tell you a lot more.

t_ch: 我想多听听你扯闲篇…
t_en: I want to hear more of your gossip...

t_ch: 有没有什么陈年旧梦的八卦？
t_en: Is there any old gossip?

t_ch: 这个话题我有发言权。
t_en: I am the best qualified to talk about this.

t_ch: 伯纳德你认识吧？就是地概科那个倔老头，一看就不好惹， 眉毛比眼睛都有精神的那个。
t_en: You know Bernard, don't you? The stubborn old guy from the Department of Cosmic Geography who doesn't look very approachable. His eyebrows are fiercer than his eyes.

t_ch: 你别看他现在这么阻挠他徒弟的爱情，当年的他，好家伙，那叫一个多情郎啊。
t_en: Don't be fooled by his seeming determination to prevent his apprentice from dating. You never could've guessed that he used to be quite the womanizer back in his younger days.

t_ch: 年轻的时候吧，这伯纳德情窦初开，想要追密卷科一个姑娘。我们这些做兄弟的能不帮忙吗？那前前后后，忙了好一哆嗦。
t_en: Back when he was young, Bernard fell in love for the first time with a girl from the Department of Insight. As his brothers, we all helped him to pursue her for a while.

t_ch: 这年轻时候的爱情就是炽热，我怀疑伯纳德那老东西，就是在洛奇身上，看到了自个儿年轻时候的影子吧，嘿。
t_en: Young love is always full of passion. I guess Bernard just saw his younger self in Rocky, haha.

t_ch: 啊？你问我之后怎么着了？年轻人，这听八卦可不兴一口气听完。想要知道，就请听下回分解吧！
t_en: What's that? You want to know what happened then? Kiddo, I can't spill all the beans in one go. Stay tuned!

t_ch: 有没有什么难以忘怀的故事？
t_en: Any unforgettable stories?

t_ch: 到了我这把岁数看过去，那啥事都多少有点难以忘怀，你懂这意思吧？
t_en: At my age, everything is unforgettable. You know what I mean?

t_ch: 真不是我说，你们年轻人其实不爱听这句，但我还是得说——没有啥忘不掉的事儿，没有啥过不去的坎儿！真要是有，那就是当时没琢磨明白，当局者迷了！
t_en: I know young people these days hate to hear this, but nothing is truly unforgettable, and there's nothing you can't get over! If there is, it's only because you've been too lost to figure it out!

t_ch: 被你这么一问，我脑子里还真糊涂起来了。一下子好多事儿都冲出来了。真是不能提，不能想啊。
t_en: Now that you brought it up, I am actually confused. I just remembered so many things all at once. The past really doesn't bear being remembered.

t_ch: 什么古恩那家伙年轻的时候风风火火的样子，还有伯纳德刚来的时候那身板才叫一个结实……
t_en: How energetic Gunn used to be... And how strong Bernard was when he first joined us...

t_ch: 哎，物是人非咯！
t_en: *sigh* How things have changed!

t_ch: 你最近都在忙什么呢。
t_en: What have you been working on?

t_ch: 退休之后就不能说自己在忙什么了，一般都说是：最近在玩儿着什么呢。
t_en: I'm retired, so that might not be an accurate question. We normally just say what we've been playing with.

t_ch: 我没啥文化，不像是古恩看得明白愚人戏。我大半辈子都搞机修，和载具称兄道弟，老了也只会折腾点这个。
t_en: I never got a proper education. I wouldn't understand Fool's Play like Gunn does. My life's work has been all about repairing and handling machines and vehicles, and now that I'm old, that's still the only thing I do.

t_ch: 喏，瞧见没，这个小玩具。可别觉得是给小孩儿玩的！这辆装甲车跑过的路，比我喝的酒都多！这个小模型，里头完美复刻了原型的机动轨道呢。
t_en: Here, check this out. Don't think this is some child's plaything! The armored car this miniature model is based on has traveled on more roads than I could count! This model even has a perfect replica of the real thing's mechanized track.

t_ch: 我当年可是修了它足足三次，三次都是从濒死边缘把它救回来……
t_en: I fixed this thing three times. Three times I managed to bring it back from the brink of death ...

t_ch: 哎哟，不说了不说了，都是过去的事儿咯！
t_en: Well, I think I'm done talking. These stories are all water under the bridge.

t_ch: 没什么别的想问了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 和你扯闲篇，够劲儿，开心！年轻人，下次有空再来，别的没有，故事管够！
t_en: It was fun chatting with you. I liked it! Come here again when you have time, kiddo. I might not have much in my possession, but I sure have more than enough stories to share.

t_ch: 那个臭小子，又开始在那里折腾那些破石头，真不知道把自己的前途放到哪儿去！
t_en: That boy is playing with his stupid rocks again. I have no idea what he wants to do with his future!

t_ch: 再这么下去，也不能怪其他科室看不起咱们地概科。如此堕落！还怎么能有成就啊！
t_en: If he keeps doing this, we can't blame the other departments for looking down on us. He's hopeless! How is he going to achieve anything?

t_ch: 哎！男女之情，风花雪月，简直就是科研求知道路上的阻挠啊！
t_en: Sigh! The fleeting romance between men and women is an obstruction to the way of scientific research!

t_ch: 其他科室为什么看不起地概科？
t_en: Why is Dpt. of Cosmic Geography looked down on?

t_ch: 嘿，你这小子，新来的吧？这种问题，你居然问我伯纳德，真不知道是故意找骂，还是太傻太天真！
t_en: Hey, kiddo, are you new here? How dare you ask me this question? Are you looking for trouble? Or are you an idiot?

t_ch: 算了，看你一脸呆样，恐怕也不是那些有坏心思的人。
t_en: Fine. Looking at your face, I assume you are not a bad person.

t_ch: 这个空间站有个最愚蠢的规则，就是把人才与知识，按照死板的科室进行划分。
t_en: The stupidest rule of the space station is to divide talents and knowledge rigidly based on departments.

t_ch: 第二愚蠢的规则——这是一条隐性规则：那就是将不同科室之间，分个高低贵贱、三六九等！
t_en: The second stupidest rule, which is a hidden rule, is that different departments are ranked on a hierarchy!

t_ch: 能言善辩之人则光鲜亮丽受人敬仰，沉默少言深耕研究却被人称作古板无用。这就是这个空间站的，最应该变革的地方！
t_en: Eloquent people are well respected while quiet diligent researchers are called old-fashioned and stubborn. This is what the space station should change the most!

t_ch: 咳咳……不过我也这把老骨头，搞什么变革也不是我有能力的。真要说，还是年轻人自己努力才行啊。
t_en: Ehem... But I'm just an old guy. It's not up to me to change anything. But if you ask me, young people should work hard to achieve what they want.

t_ch: 那些破石头是什么？
t_en: What are those rocks?

t_ch: 哼，那些小玩意儿，是我徒弟的情感寄托！真是肉麻。
t_en: Hmph, those little things are my student's emotional sustenance. Gross.

t_ch: 人家莱斯莉，那么年轻有作为，本琥珀纪唯一一个去丰饶星域做科研的，哪儿会看得上他这么个地概科小科员啊？
t_en: Lesley is so young and promising. She's the only person who went to the Abundance segmentum to do research in this Amber Era. She won't even bat her eyes at an ordinary researcher from the DGG.

t_ch: 不过是平日研究的时候有过几次交际，这小子就忘乎所以了！
t_en: They only had a few interactions during their research, and he's already gotten ahead of himself!

t_ch: 还在人家莱斯莉离开空间站之后，搞了一堆稀奇古怪的小石头，在上面雕刻花纹，说是要做一个莱斯莉出来。真的疯了！
t_en: He made a bunch of weird little rocks while Lesley is away. He carved the rocks, saying that's Lesley's face. He's really out of his mind!

t_ch: 他但凡拿出雕刻石头的心思，到真正的科研上去，做出和莱斯莉一样的成就来，那才叫一个厉害！哎，这小子，真是气死我了。
t_en: If only he had spent his effort carving rocks into his research and made achievements like Lesley! Hmph! He's killing me!

t_ch: 我还有些问题想问…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 你认为莱斯莉喜欢洛奇吗？
t_en: Do you think Lesley likes Rocky?

t_ch: 要我说，那就是癞蛤蟆想吃天鹅肉！做梦去吧。
t_en: If you ask me, that can only be his wishful thinking! That can only happen in his dreams!

t_ch: 或许是因为关于那个姑娘的传闻太多，以至于大家都忘记了一个最关键的事实。那就是她自己本身的个人能力，是多么的超凡绝伦。
t_en: Maybe there are so many rumors about that girl that everyone has forgotten one important fact — her individual capacities are simply extraordinary.

t_ch: 莱斯莉不仅仅是一个明星那么简单。她是一个天才学者，是前期的基因与后期的努力共同作用出来的成果，即便是她的妹妹埃美丽，也绝对不会有她的成就。
t_en: Lesley is more than a celebrity. She is a gifted scholar. She's the combined result of her genes and her own efforts. Even her sister Emily won't be able to achieve anything like that.

t_ch: 如果说莱斯莉是百年一遇的天才，那洛奇这小子就是随处可见的科员。
t_en: Lesley is a once-in-a-century genius while Rocky is only an ordinary researcher.

t_ch: 你说说，她会看上他吗？
t_en: Tell me, do you think she'll like him?

t_ch: 你支持他们在一起吗？
t_en: Will you support them being together?

t_ch: 这种话我怎么好评价？你会思考如果自己有幸加入天才俱乐部，要进行什么样子的自我介绍吗？
t_en: How am I supposed to comment on something like that? Would you prepare for an opening speech for the Genius Society? You won't!

t_ch: 那就压根儿是不可能的事儿！
t_en: It's just something impossible!

t_ch: 如果莱斯莉真的回应了洛奇的感情，那洛奇可要加把劲儿。不然两个人的差距太大，生活方式都不同，就算在一起了，以后也会吵架。
t_en: If Lesley really says yes to Rocky, he'll have to work hard. Otherwise, the gap between them will be too big and their lifestyles will be too different. Even if they are together, they will fight a lot.

t_ch: 我人老了，最看不得那种悲欢离别。真要是想长久在一起，洛奇这小子，打铁还需自身硬！
t_en: I'm old. I can't bear to see people break up. If they want to be together in the long run, Rocky will have to toughen up himself!

t_ch: 咳咳……我都说了些什么！那都是空谈，空谈！
t_en: Ehem... Look what I said! Nonsense! Nonsense!

t_ch: 如何评价这次的入侵。
t_en: How do you evaluate the invasion?

t_ch: 意料之中的事情。无论是入侵，还是能够守住空间站，都不令人意外。
t_en: Expected, both for the invasion itself and the survival of the space station.

t_ch: 任何外部攻击都是难以击垮空间站的，这一点我深信不疑。
t_en: I had no doubt that any external attack will be unable to destroy the space station.

t_ch: 因为我知道，更为严重的症结，并不在于外部的纷扰，而是内部的扭曲。
t_en: Because I know the worse issue lies in internal distortion instead of external disturbance.

t_ch: 要我说，科室之间的矛盾与鄙视链再这么下去，我老头子还没彻底合眼，恐怕就能看见这座空间站的覆灭——因为自己的问题，内部的瓦解！
t_en: If conflicts and contempts continue to exist among different departments, I'm afraid the space station will collapse before I die. Its own issue leads to its internal disintegration!

t_ch: 我没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 你也是年轻人，比我这种老头子说话更有感染力。你要是有空，也去劝劝我那个傻徒弟。
t_en: You are a youngster too. Your words are more influential than mine. When you have time, please talk to my student.

t_ch: 哎，真是我的不幸，更是地概科的不幸啊！
t_en: Sigh. It's my misfortune as well as the Department of Cosmic Geography's misfortune!

t_ch: 哎，都怪这场入侵，搞得咱们科室好多文件又得重新整理了……
t_en: Sigh. Thanks to this invasion, I have to re-arrange a lot of the files of our department.

t_ch: 现在的年轻科员，又静不下性子来做整理的活儿。哎，最后还得是我这把老骨头，来收拾残局。
t_en: Young researchers nowadays never have the patience to organize things. Old people like me always have to stay behind to clean up the mess.

t_ch: 嗯…那就一三五让古恩来整理，二四六让温天翁来整理吧！
t_en: Well... I'll ask Gunn to come in the even days of the week and Wen Tianweng to come in the odd days of the week!

t_ch: 要不我来帮帮忙吧？
t_en: I can help too!

t_ch: 哎哟，好乖的年轻科员。这么热心肠的孩子，我可是不多见了。
t_en: Oh, what a good kid! Not many kids these days are as kind as you are.

t_ch: 先自我介绍一下吧，我是密卷科的以斯帖，如您所见，并不是个年轻的科员。平日里做的工作，主要还是整理文档方面。
t_en: Let me introduce myself first. I'm Esther from the Department of Insight. As you can see, I'm not a young researcher. I mainly take care of the filing.

t_ch: 如果有什么关于密卷科文档的事情想要咨询，我应该还能帮上点忙。
t_en: If you have any questions about files of the Department of Insight, I should be able to help you.

t_ch: 你看上去很年轻啊。
t_en: You look very young.

t_ch: 哎哟哟，嘴真甜。你们这帮年轻人真会说话。
t_en: Aww, that's sweet. Young people these days know how to talk.

t_ch: 虽然我也不是那种不服老的人，但平日里的护理保养，我确实一样没落下。
t_en: Although I'm content with my age, I never skip a single step in my skincare routine.

t_ch: 你们年轻人赶时髦的那些玩意儿，我也平时留心关注着。喏，你看我今天的口红色号，是不是不一般？星际流光系列的最新雾面唇釉，好看吧。
t_en: I pay attention to all the fashionable things young people do. Just look at my lipstick color. It's quite special, isn't it? It's the latest matte lipstick from the Galaxy Luminous collection. It looks good, right?

t_ch: 我还有些问题想要问…
t_en: I've got something to ask you.

t_ch: 这场入侵对密卷科影响是最大的吗？
t_en: Does the Department of Insight suffer the most from this invasion?

t_ch: 我不了解其他科室的情况，但是单说密卷科，这场入侵无疑是一场灾难。
t_en: I'm not sure about the other departments. But this invasion is undoubtedly a disaster for us.

t_ch: 我们有大量的资料被摧毁，它们不同于实验材料，可以凭借复刻一次实验就得到数据。里面的许多星神秘闻，是很难有机会再次听闻的……
t_en: A lot of our files were destroyed. Unlike experimental documents, which they can get the data by repeating the experiment, many Aeon mysteries will be lost forever...

t_ch: 之后或许我还需要联络一些密卷科的老同事，一起想办法转录一些材料。只有存放在大脑里的东西，或许才是最长久的吧。
t_en: I may have to contact some ex-colleagues to transcribe some of the files. Things stored in our heads last the longest.

t_ch: 有没有什么我可以帮到你的？
t_en: Is there anything I can help with?

t_ch: 乖孩子，你看上去并不是密卷科的成员。我不能这样随意麻烦你。
t_en: You're such a good kid. You don't look like you are from the Department of Insight. I shouldn't trouble you.

t_ch: 而且现如今人力也还算充足。我还有一些老朋友可以为我提供援助。
t_en: We've got enough people. A few of my old friends are willing to help.

t_ch: 如果等到之后，就连温天翁那个结实的身板都忙得受不了，那我再来麻烦你这位小年轻吧。
t_en: If even someone as strong as Wen Tianweng couldn't handle this, I guess I'll have to seek help from you.

t_ch: 你的时尚品味很赞。
t_en: You have great fashion sense.

t_ch: 哎哟哎哟，说一次就好啦。一直这样夸，我也会不好意思的。
t_en: Oh, I am flattered. I'll be embarrassed if you keep talking like that.

t_ch: 不过你能看出我身上的穿搭风格与妆容安排，想来也是一位关注时尚的小年轻呢。
t_en: However, I guess you are a stylish youngster yourself if you can see my fashion style and my makeup.

t_ch: 每天收拾打扮得称心如意，是我保持活力与开心的秘诀，偷偷分享给你吧。
t_en: Dressing up every day is my secret to staying energetic and happy. This is my secret.

t_ch: 我没什么要问的啦。
t_en: That's all I have to ask.

t_ch: 如果之后有什么问题，可以随时来问我。
t_en: Feel free to come to me if you have any questions.

t_ch: 找到属于自己的风格了吗？
t_en: Have you found your own fashion style?

t_ch: 准确来说还在摸索当中。之前总是关注各类品牌的最新款，现在要把这个毛病改过来，还是挺费劲的。
t_en: To be honest, I'm still looking. It's a bit difficult to fix my bad habit of only paying attention to the latest products.

t_ch: 不过大方向上已经决定好了。这次我不会再陷入「流行时尚」的怪圈了。这还得给你说一声谢谢呢，小年轻。
t_en: But I have a general direction now. I won't fall into the trap of modern fashion again and I have you to thank for that, young one.

t_ch: 抱歉，我刚才专心在安排工作，没注意到你来了。
t_en: Sorry, I was busy arranging work for my team. Didn't notice you were here.

t_ch: 是有什么事情要问我吗？艾丝妲小姐吩咐过了，我会尽力配合你和列车组工作的。
t_en: Ask me anything. I'll do my best to help as per Lady Asta's request.

t_ch: 那我可就不客气了…
t_en: Anything? Hmm...

t_ch: 你、你也别问太奇怪的问题！超过我认知范围的事情我没办法回答。
t_en: J—Just nothing too weird! I can only tell you what I know.

t_ch: 有没有打算扩招防卫科？
t_en: Any plans to hire more hands for the Security Department?

t_ch: 这次「军团」入侵确实暴露了防卫科人员不足的事实，但是扩招的话涉及资金流动和人员管理，具体还是需要由艾丝妲小姐批准。
t_en: With this Legion invasion, it's become very apparent that we're short-staffed. But getting more people in will require proper funding and additional administration work. I'd have to get the green light from Lady Asta on these matters.

t_ch: 我能做的部分，主要还是尽可能培训目前已有的这一批防卫科科员。平时空间站的氛围比较平稳，除了处理科员的研究事故之外，防卫科的工作也就只有普通巡逻了。
t_en: What I can do at this stage is to step up the training for our existing staff members. Most of the time there's not much work for us at the station. We just need to handle the occasional research accidents and do our daily patrolling.

t_ch: 归根结底，还是我做得不够好，太安于现状，没有危机意识了……
t_en: At the end of the day, I'm the one to be blamed for the invasion. I was too complacent and failed to detect a major threat...

t_ch: 除了工作你都不休息吗？
t_en: Do you ever have any personal time?

t_ch: 休息？哦…工作之外的时间我也有自己的私事要处理。主要是个人体能训练和资料学习。
t_en: Personal time? Oh... I do have things to take care of outside work, mostly studying and training.

t_ch: 除此以外，我偶尔也会协助艾丝妲小姐处理一些事务。不过我不太擅长文书类的工作，这方面我还需要想办法提升，这样才能帮到小姐。
t_en: I'm also kind of a personal assistant to Lady Asta. That said, I'd need to improve my writing skills to be of more help.

t_ch: 你为什么要呆在空间站？
t_en: Why did you choose to work at the space station?

t_ch: 这…小姐在这里，我自然也就在这里。
t_en: I... I follow Lady Asta's steps.

t_ch: 我没有想过去往小姐不在的地方。更何况在空间站，我可以发挥自己的作用和价值。维护这里的安宁，看到科员安心地完成自己的研究…我就心满意足了。
t_en: I don't see myself choosing otherwise. Besides, I think my work here is meaningful. I'm happy to see our researchers conducting their work in a safe environment.

t_ch: 我没什么要问的了。
t_en: That's all from me.

t_ch: 不打扰你工作了。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 好的，那我就继续工作了。要是你对防卫科事务有什么投诉意见，可以随时来找我。
t_en: Thanks. Oh, and come find me anytime you have feedback for the Security Department.

t_ch: 抱歉，我刚才专心在安排工作，没有注意到你过来了。
t_en: Sorry, I was busy arranging work for my team. Didn't notice you were here.

t_ch: 是有什么事情要问我吗？艾丝妲小姐吩咐过了，我会尽力配合你和列车组工作的。
t_en: Ask me anything. I'll do my best to help as per Lady Asta's request.

t_ch: 从心、从镜、从我…
t_en: Of the heart, of the mirror, of the self...

t_ch: 你！你突然说些什么！抱歉…刚才反应有点激动，但这种话在公共场合讲出口也太奇怪了！
t_en: S—Stop saying that! Sorry, I don't mean to yell... But still, it's embarrassing to mention it in front of everyone!

t_ch: 你就是雄鹰中的骏马？
t_en: So you're the horse among eagles?

t_ch: 这都是那帮臭小子随口胡说的，你别当真。在战斗技巧这方面，我还有很长的路要走。
t_en: They were just talking nonsense. I still have a long way to go to hone my combat skills.

t_ch: 另外…也请你不要给艾丝妲小姐讲这个名字。实在是…太…太羞耻了……
t_en: Also... please don't tell Lady Asta about the horse thing. It's just ridiculous.

t_ch: 没事别乱捡地上的东西。
t_en: Guess the moral is "don't pick up just anything you find on the floor."

t_ch: 哎，这次的确是我草率了。我会吸取教训，下次查阅典籍一定翻找出处明晰的那种，不会再相信这种路边小纸条了。
t_en: Yeah, I should've known better. I'll definitely use more reputable resources next time. I won't fall for roadside leaflets again.

t_ch: 不过我听人说过…你好像也经常这么干？总之，你也要多加小心。
t_en: Although I did hear that you like collecting those leaflets as well. Better be careful.

t_ch: 黑塔女士没拿你怎么着吧？
t_en: Did Madam Herta give you some attitude?

t_ch: …那件事还是别提了。黑塔女士…她做事自有她的主张。
t_en: ...I don't really want to talk about it. Madam Herta does things her own way. I'll say that much.

t_ch: 而且艾丝妲小姐也信任着黑塔女士，我没有理由…去质疑她的行为，嗯。
t_en: Lady Asta trusts her, so I don't have any reason to, uh, question why she's doing what she's doing.

t_ch: 佩佩也未免太机灵了吧！
t_en: Peppy sure is smart!

t_ch: 所有见过它的人都这么说。作为宠物狗，佩佩不仅有自己的情绪，它还能观察到周围人的情绪。
t_en: Right? That's what people say! Peppy has a personality. He can sense other people's needs, too.

t_ch: 比如有一次深夜，艾丝妲小姐处理工作事务太累，一不小心在办公桌上睡着了。佩佩它独自跑过来找我，把我带到小姐那里，我才能提醒小姐上床休息，避免着凉感冒。
t_en: One time, it was late at night, and Lady Asta fell asleep on her desk after a day's hard work. Peppy came to me and led me to Lady Asta so that I could escort her back to her room.

t_ch: 这次也是要多谢佩佩，才能让小姐找到解决问题的方法。看来之后也得多多感谢它才行啊。
t_en: Peppy saved the day again this time. Thanks to him, Lady Asta was able to work things out. I'll have to give him a treat.

t_ch: 我还是不欺负你了。
t_en: You know what? I'll stop making fun of you.

t_ch: …原来你刚才是在拿我打趣吗？
t_en: Huh? Was that what you were doing...?

t_ch: 总之这次的事件十分感谢你愿意帮助小姐。如果之后还有特殊情况，也希望你可以为她伸出援手。当然…这次的这种事件还是别再发生了。
t_en: Anyways, thanks for helping Lady Asta out. I hope you'll lend a hand again when she finds herself in difficult situations in the future. That said, the fewer the situations, the better...

t_ch: 算了，不逗你了。
t_en: Fine, fine, I'll stop messing with you.

t_ch: 好的，那我就继续工作了。要是你对防卫科事务有什么投诉意见，可以随时来找我。
t_en: Thanks. Oh, and come find me anytime you have feedback for the Security Department.

t_ch: 密钥的事情有后续吗？
t_en: Any follow-ups on the encryption key incident?

t_ch: 想不到你还记得啊。哎，小姐一直都有这个坏习惯，消费起来有点大手大脚，要是把密钥还给她，真不知道她会买些什么东西回来。
t_en: Ah, you still remember. Well, Lady Asta is known to spend more than what she could bring home. Who knows what she would buy had she gotten the encryption key?

t_ch: 上次多亏了有你的帮助，不仅解决了匿名群聊的事情，还帮小姐守住了秘密，有机会我一定要重谢你才行。
t_en: Thanks to your help last time, the trouble with the anonymous group chat has been resolved, and Lady Asta got to keep her secret. I've got to repay you sometime.

t_ch: 让我摸摸佩佩吧！
t_en: Let me pet Peppy!

t_ch: 这倒是不难。佩佩和很多科员都相处得不错。
t_en: That can be arranged. Peppy gets along with people pretty well.

t_ch: 不过这件事还是需要佩佩自己同意才行，我也得尊重它的想法。
t_en: But Peppy has the final say in this. I have to respect his opinion.

t_ch: 密钥有消费限额吗？
t_en: Does the encryption key have a credit cap?

t_ch: 我不知道，但小姐那边还没有出现过密钥无法支付的情况。
t_en: I'm not sure. But so far Lady Asta has encountered no problems using it to make payments.

t_ch: 我再琢磨琢磨吧。
t_en: I'll have to think about it.

t_ch: 不着急，我也要好好想一想。
t_en: Same. Take your time.

t_ch: 复兴计划路漫漫啊…
t_en: Looks like Project Revival has a long way to go...

t_ch: 我已经收到对应科员的回复。多亏了你的帮助，才能顺利完成这轮的复兴计划。就连辛克尔夫人都对你夸赞连连！
t_en: I've got some replies from the researchers. Thanks for helping me with this round of Project Revival. Even Mrs. Hinkel has started to sing your praise!

t_ch: 这次真的多谢你了。有了你的协助，相信空间站的运作一定可以很快恢复到正常状态的！
t_en: Thank you so much. With your help, the operations on the space station will soon be back on track!

t_ch: 哼哼，我对你还有点兴趣，有什么要问的就问。
t_en: Hmph, I haven't completely lost my interest in you. Ask what you gotta ask.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: Well...

t_ch: 有话就直说。
t_en: Just say it.

t_ch: 你到底有多少个人偶？
t_en: How many puppets do you have?

t_ch: 常驻<unbreak>249</unbreak>个，备用<unbreak>32</unbreak>个。我懒得操纵人偶走路。
t_en: There are <unbreak>249</unbreak> in service and another <unbreak>32</unbreak> as backups. I can't be bothered to control their walking, so I just keep plenty around in each area.

t_ch: 你在空间站里收藏了什么？
t_en: What's in your collection here on the station?

t_ch: 一些还算有意思的奇物，一些没什么意思的人。
t_en: Curios that are mildly interesting and people who are utterly boring.

t_ch: 你有很多奇物，能送我一个吗？
t_en: You seem to own a lot of Curios. Can I have one?

t_ch: 你会跟无期徒刑的犯人说你刑期长，能分我几年吗？
t_en: Would you ask someone who's sentenced to life to share some prison time with you just because that person can?

t_ch: 会的话，你很有趣；不会的话，你很聪明。
t_en: If you would, it means you're funny. If you wouldn't, it means you're smart.

t_ch: 这个空间站是怎么运作的？
t_en: Why do you own this space station and how do you run it?

t_ch: 公司愿意砸钱，我点了个头，就这么简单。具体运作我不知道，你得问艾丝妲。
t_en: The IPC doesn't mind another investment and I didn't reject their offer. As for its day-to-day, you need to ask Asta.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 那我还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll stop bothering you.

t_ch: 嗯，随便你。
t_en: Yep, yep, whatever.

t_ch: 空间站里有你的匿名粉丝群。
t_en: Your fans on the station have an anonymous group chat.

t_ch: 当粉丝还要匿名，他们活着可真累啊。
t_en: Even fans need to be anonymous... Kids these days.

t_ch: 但群聊被解散了。
t_en: But it has been dissolved.

t_ch: 缺乏约束的秩序，崩溃起来是很快的。
t_en: Groups with no discipline tend to collapse very easily.

t_ch: 搞不懂他们喜欢你什么。
t_en: I don't get why they like you.

t_ch: 如果喜欢需要理由，世界就一团糟了，很多人会发现自己不配被喜欢。
t_en: If people need solid reasons to like one another, the world would be plunging into chaos. Soon they'll discover that they don't deserve to be liked.

t_ch: 群里有一场闹剧…
t_en: There was some drama in that group...

t_ch: 闹就闹吧，我又不是他们的监护人。
t_en: Why should I care? I'm not their mom.

t_ch: 没什么了，只是随口一提。
t_en: Just a random thing I thought I'd mention.

t_ch: 哦，知道了。
t_en: Okay.

t_ch: 阿兰其实很怕你。
t_en: You know, Arlan is afraid of you.

t_ch: 总是跟着艾丝妲的那个小子？无所谓，我不在乎。
t_en: The boy that tags along with Asta all the time? Okay, whatever.

t_ch: 他觉得面对你很有压力。
t_en: He feels great pressure when you are present.

t_ch: 噢，不影响他为我办事就行。
t_en: Doesn't matter. As long as he keeps running errands for me.

t_ch: 他不知道该怎么和你相处。
t_en: He doesn't know how to get along with you.

t_ch: 那也这么相处过来了，不是吗？他会学会的。
t_en: And yet he survived, no? He'll figure it out.

t_ch: 如何评价阿兰。
t_en: What do you think of Arlan?

t_ch: 平时人挺老实的，吩咐他做点小事也很麻利。
t_en: Honest fella, does what he's told.

t_ch: 那没事了。
t_en: That's all I wanted to ask.

t_ch: 哦，知道了。
t_en: Okay.

t_ch: 别盯着我的人偶看！你的鼻孔很漂亮吗？
t_en: Stop staring at my puppet all the time! I don't find the view of your nostrils that enjoyable.

t_ch: 没事做的话就去测试「模拟宇宙」，别带着星核在空间站里乱窜，炸了怎么办。
t_en: If you don't have anything better to do, go test my Simulated Universe. Quit wandering about the station carrying a Stellaron. People will have to wipe you down everywhere if it explodes.

t_ch: 人偶没有回应，看来黑塔本人并不在线。
t_en: No reaction from the puppet, looks like Herta hasn't come online yet.

t_ch: 看你好像很闲，不如过来配合我做个「小」实验吧？
t_en: You look like you need work. How about assisting me with a tiny little experiment?

t_ch: 有事说事，别挡我视线。
t_en: State your business and stop blocking my view.

t_ch: 是你呀，空间站的事情多亏了你的帮忙。
t_en: It's you! Thanks for helping out on the station.

t_ch: 怎么了，是遇到什么麻烦了吗？
t_en: Did you encounter some problems?

t_ch: 我想找你聊聊…
t_en: Can we talk...?

t_ch: 可以呀，你想聊些什么？
t_en: Of course. What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 关于你的工作…
t_en: About your job...

t_ch: 我的工作？其实就是帮黑塔女士代为打理一二，并不算难啦。
t_en: My job? I keep things running on Madam Herta's behalf. It's not that difficult really.

t_ch: 你怎么不待在办公室里？
t_en: Why aren't you staying in your office?
